response:
Sec. 1
Original events in Lekki on November 20, 2020, marked a tragic turning point for Nigeria when soldiers opened fire on peaceful protesters.

Sec. 2
Original footage captured the chaotic scene as young people were running in fear while military personnel discharged their weapons without warning.

Sec. 3
Original reports from the ground described how the protesters were demanding the release of detained activists and better living conditions for the youth.

Sec. 4
Original witnesses stated that the soldiers advanced aggressively toward the crowd, ignoring the non-violent nature of the demonstration that day.

Sec. 5
Original investigations by human rights groups later confirmed that several lives were lost due to the excessive use of force by the state security apparatus.

Sec. 6
Original calls for justice have continued for years as families of the victims seek accountability and truth about the circumstances of the Lekki massacre.

Sec. 7
Original memories of that fateful night remain etched in the minds of Nigerians, serving as a somber reminder of the cost of political dissent.
